page: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores with hyper-threading running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 6 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1892.28 and a multi-core score of 15466.97. Memory bandwidth tests show 828.39 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 97.52 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database and key-value store tests, the instance handles 15.88 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 7564.77 on PassMark database operations.

While the instance performs well in memory-bound tasks and standard database operations, it exhibits limitations in heavy computational tasks. For example, LLM inference prompt processing with a medium 7B model drops to a weak rating at 31.86 tokens/sec, and there is no GPU acceleration. However, the combination of 128 GB RAM, dedicated CPU allocation, and 950 GB of high-speed local NVMe storage makes this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, caching layers, and data indexing without the overhead of external network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average tier performance, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 15466.97 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.88 million operations per second. While it lacks GPU acceleration and shows weaker performance on larger LLM inference tasks, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage provide strong resource density. This configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers balanced performance for database operations and memory-intensive applications.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR4 RAM, and integrated NVMe SSD local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM delivering an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
Bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store for high-speed temporary storage.
Intel Xeon 8375C processor with dedicated core allocation at 3.5 GHz.
Average performance tier across database, Redis, and memory bandwidth benchmarks.
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data processing.
